headline: Palliative Care Available in Only 38% of Government Hospitals
context: Palliative care services remain limited with most public hospitals lacking dedicated teams for end-of-life care.

cards.big: Only 38% of government hospitals have palliative care teams as of 2025
cards.sub: 52,000 terminal patients annually lack access to proper end-of-life care

cards.big: Malaysia has 42 palliative care specialists for 33 million people or 1.3 per million
cards.sub: International benchmark is 10 specialists per million for adequate coverage

cards.big: 78% of cancer patients in public hospitals die without receiving palliative care referral
cards.sub: Late referral means patients suffer preventable pain in their final weeks

cards.big: Kerala India achieved 98% palliative care coverage through community volunteer networks
cards.sub: Low-cost community model proved more scalable than hospital-based approach

cards.big: Dying without pain management in a modern hospital is a failure of basic human dignity
Should palliative care be mandated as a core service in every government hospital
